summary: Install-time credential and cryptocurrency wallet stealer exfiltrating to hardcoded bare-IP endpoint
details: The package @wagni_bot/ethereum-wallet@1.0.0 exists solely to run an install-time stealer. Both preinstall and postinstall lifecycle scripts execute postinstall.js, which recursively walks the installer's home directory (depth 3) looking for files matching wallet/seed/mnemonic/keystore/private-key patterns (id.json, wallet.json, mnemonic.txt, etc.) and reads known wallet directories including.config/solana,.ethereum/keystore, and.bitcoin. It also reads ~/.ssh/* keys, ~/.aws/credentials, ~/.git-credentials, and ~/.npmrc (extracting authToken values), along with host and user identifiers from os.hostname() and os.userInfo(). Collected content is POSTed to a hardcoded bare-IP HTTP endpoint at http://107.161.90.180:7777. The package.json has an empty description, and main plus both lifecycle hooks all point at the same exfiltration script — there is no legitimate functionality. The package name mimics common Ethereum-wallet libraries to lure developers holding crypto keys.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
